title: Kubernetes Foundations Knowledge Check
description: Test your understanding of core Kubernetes concepts covered in the Introduction and Architecture workshops.

questions.question: What is the smallest deployable unit in Kubernetes?
options: A. Container
B. Pod
C. Deployment
D. Node
questions.explanation: A Pod is the smallest deployable unit. It wraps one or more containers that share network and storage namespaces. You cannot deploy a bare container in Kubernetes — it must be inside a Pod.

questions.question: A Deployment has 3 replicas. You delete one Pod manually. What happens?
options: A. The Deployment scales down to 2 replicas permanently
B. The cluster raises an alert but takes no action
C. The ReplicaSet controller creates a replacement Pod to restore 3 replicas
D. The node where the Pod ran is cordoned
questions.explanation: The ReplicaSet controller continuously reconciles actual state to desired state. When a Pod is deleted, the controller detects the deficit and creates a new Pod immediately.

questions.question: Which kubectl command shows the events associated with a Pod, including scheduling decisions and image pull errors?
options: A. kubectl get pod <name> -o yaml
B. kubectl logs <name>
C. kubectl describe pod <name>
D. kubectl top pod <name>
questions.explanation: kubectl describe pod shows the full object status including the Events section at the bottom. This is the primary debugging tool for understanding why a Pod is stuck.

questions.question: What is the purpose of a Kubernetes Service?
options: A. To build container images on the cluster
B. To provide a stable DNS name and virtual IP for a set of Pods
C. To schedule Pods onto specific nodes
D. To store sensitive configuration data
questions.explanation: Services provide a stable ClusterIP and DNS name that load-balances across healthy Pod endpoints. Without a Service, callers would need to track individual Pod IPs, which change every time a Pod is restarted.

questions.question: Which liveness probe behaviour causes Kubernetes to restart a container?
options: A. The probe succeeds on the first attempt
B. The probe fails consecutively more times than the failureThreshold
C. The probe takes longer than 30 seconds to return
D. The probe endpoint returns HTTP 200
questions.explanation: When a liveness probe fails consecutively beyond the failureThreshold (default: 3), the kubelet kills and restarts the container. This handles deadlocked processes that are running but not healthy.
